title: Encyclia euosma
intro: Encyclia euosma is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and protect against oxidative stress. Bioactive constituents of Encyclia euosma include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Encyclia euosma is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use is not well-studied and should be approached with caution.
taxonomy: Encyclia euosma is classified within the plant kingdom as a member of the family Orchidaceae. This family is commonly known as the orchid family. The genus Encyclia is further classified within the subfamily Epidendroideae. The subfamily Epidendroideae is a large and diverse group of orchids that includes many species. Encyclia euosma is a specific species within this broader classification.
